mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from an already-low level, the market reads easier policy as a lower discount-rate regime and a backstop against downside growth surprises, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during months when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the sign advantage disappears in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in CPIAUCSL indicates inflation pressure that raises expected policy restraint and real-rate risk, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days once the inflation acceleration threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPIAUCSL spikes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average 5-day return after triggers is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS with no concurrent increase in UNRATE signals broadening labor income and household demand, which supports corporate revenues and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-flat condition does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of instances, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS reflects stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, so when retail sales accelerate for two consecutive months, equity prices should continue higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month RSAFS acceleration is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns at least as often as positive returns, or if the signal loses predictive value after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weaker current output and margin pressure from underutilized capacity, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days when industrial production is falling on a trend basis.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average 60-day return after INDPRO downtrends is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A low but rising HOUST series signals housing-cycle improvement and stronger demand spillovers into consumer and financial equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days after a housing trough is confirmed.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises 2 months after a trough
failure_condition: Reject if post-trough HOUST rebounds are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises while PAYEMS falls, the labor market is deteriorating fast and earnings expectations weaken, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS falls
failure_condition: Reject if the combined unemployment-up/payroll-down condition does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of occurrences, or if either leg alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rising CPIAUCSL combined with stable or higher FEDFUNDS tightens real financial conditions and pressures equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-plus-policy-tightening episodes are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term becomes insignificant in any 2-year rolling window.
